subject: Computer Networking
title: Lazy Loading
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Lazy Loading.

hook.text: A page has 50 images but only 3 are visible. Why load all 50? Lazy loading defers the rest until needed.

buildup.text: The loading='lazy' attribute on tells the browser to load images only when they enter the viewport.

discovery.text: Intersection Observer API gives precise control. Trigger loads when elements are N pixels from view.

twist.text: Lazy load too aggressively and above-the-fold images appear blank. Exempt hero images from lazy loading.

climax.text: Code splitting lazy-loads JavaScript too. Import components only when the user navigates to them.

punchline.text: Load what's visible. Defer the rest. Faster for everyone.

quiz.question: What HTML attribute enables native lazy loading for images?
